name: Sliding Leg Curl
resistance_profile: Bodyweight leverage rises as the legs extend, loading the hamstrings hardest near full extension (long muscle length).
cues: Lie on your back, heels on a towel or sliders, hips bridged up
Slide your heels away until your legs are almost straight
Pull your heels back toward you while keeping the hips high
execution_steps: Lie on your back with heels on sliders (or a towel on a smooth floor), knees bent and hips bridged up.
Keeping your hips high, slide your heels away until your legs are almost straight.
Pull your heels back toward your glutes while maintaining the bridge.
Repeat without letting your hips drop to the floor.
good_when: You want a knee-flexion hamstring curl with no machine, such as at home.
You want a lighter regression that builds toward Nordic curls.
bad_when: Your hips drop and you lose tension (shorten the range or hold a bridge instead).
You need heavy progressive loading (a machine leg curl adds weight easily).
common_errors: Letting the hips drop to the floor mid-rep
Sliding out further than you can pull back with control
